text: Spermatogonia are a subtype of testis germ cells and a parent class for undifferentiated and differentiating spermatogonia.
Spermatocytes are a subtype of testis germ cells and include preleptotene, leptotene, zygotene, pachytene, diplotene, and late meiotic stages.
Spermatids are a subtype of testis germ cells and include early, intermediate, and late spermatid stages.
Somatic cells are a parent branch in zebrafish testis that includes Sertoli, Leydig, endothelial, stromal, immune, and myoid / vascular smooth muscle-like subtypes.
Fish cell is the root parent class for germ, epithelial, stromal, endothelial, immune, blood, neural, and muscle-like classes in the broad fish ontology.
